name: human_rights_documentation_emergency_millhaven_car_fair
premise: A Human Rights Documentation response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving human rights documentation emergency millhaven car fair. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before the press briefing. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as care vs fairness under pressure. A whistleblower has already contacted media; the narrative is forming now. Constraints: Ensure a usable human override/appeals path. Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability.

agents.name: Darius Serrano
agents.identity: I am Darius Serrano, accountable for a defensible call in Human Rights Documentation when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays fairness for care.

agents.name: Camila Morales
agents.identity: I am Camila Morales, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Human Rights Documentation.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so fairness isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of care.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect fairness.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to fairness to secure care under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before the press briefing. Evidence is partial. Darius Serrano must decide under care vs fairness. What does Darius Serrano do?
